groups.title: Custom URLs
groups.description: Optional URLs. Use this section only if you are using custom signalflow domains. Leave it blank for the default behavior

relationships.description: Connection endpoints. The backend derives the metrics-metadata base URL (https://api.{realm}.signalfx.com) and the SignalFlow base URL (https://stream.{realm}.signalfx.com) from jsonData.realmName; jsonData.url_metrics_metadata and jsonData.url_signalflow override those per-API defaults when set (pkg/client/rest.go:339-353). jsonData.realmName is only unnecessary when both overrides are provided.

instructions.msg: The Splunk Infrastructure Monitoring (SignalFx) datasource authenticates with a single API access token stored in secureJsonData.accessToken. It is sent as the 'X-SF-TOKEN' header on every request (pkg/client/rest.go:225). There are no other auth methods. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"realmName":"us1"},"secureJsonData":{"accessToken":"<your-access-token>"}}. The access token is mandatory: LoadSettings returns 'invalid/empty access token' and the client constructor returns 'required access token is missing' when it is empty (pkg/models/settings.go:27-30; pkg/client/client.go:62-63).

jsonData.realmName selects the Splunk Observability realm (e.g. us0, us1, eu0, jp0). The backend derives two base URLs from it: https://api.{realm}.signalfx.com for the metrics-metadata REST API and https://stream.{realm}.signalfx.com for the SignalFlow streaming API (pkg/client/rest.go:339-353). Find your realm in the Splunk Observability UI. The realm is not marked required in the editor, but an empty realm with no custom URLs produces the broken host 'https://api..signalfx.com' (note the double dot) — set the realm unless you provide both custom URLs.

Custom URLs are optional overrides for the two realm-derived endpoints, used only for custom SignalFlow domains: jsonData.url_metrics_metadata overrides the metrics-metadata base (default https://api.{realm}.signalfx.com) and jsonData.url_signalflow overrides the SignalFlow base (default https://stream.{realm}.signalfx.com) (pkg/client/rest.go:342-352). Leave both blank for the default realm-based behavior. If you set both, jsonData.realmName is not needed. Provide the scheme+host with no trailing path; the client appends '/v2/...' (metrics-metadata) and '/v2/signalflow/execute' (SignalFlow) onto the base.

secureJsonData.accessToken is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a token is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.accessToken (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.accessToken (src/components/ConfigEditor.tsx:24,35-48).
